question_title: Will a Turkish civil court (Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi) appoint a "call committee" (çağrı heyeti) to convene a CHP extraordinary congress before July 12, 2026?
summary: As of June 30, 2026, the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi application was announced but not confirmed filed, and no ruling had been issued. For YES, three hurdles must clear within ~12 days: (1) the application is filed, (2) the court rules before July 11, and (3) the ruling is a favorable national-level çağrı heyeti appointment (not a rejection, postponement, or interim bilirkişi-only step). Applications 'typically' take ~15 days, which would push the median ruling past the July 11 window, and the disputed signature count (833 vs 820) likely triggers a bilirkişi examination first — explicitly excluded by the fine print. Counterbalancing: Özel's camp faces a hard deadline (15-day notice for a July 26 congress) creating strong urgency for expedited handling, with clear precedents (MHP 2016, CHP İstanbul). The modal outcome remains 'court has not yet ruled by July 11.' Conditional chain ≈ 0.90 × 0.40 × 0.60 ≈ 0.22.

factors.description: Status quo: no application confirmed filed and no ruling as of June 30; resolves NO if court hasn't ruled by July 11
Tight timeline: applications typically ~15 days; if filed June 29-30, median ruling ~July 14, after the window
Likely bilirkişi signature-examination step first (disputed 833 vs 820), which fine print excludes and which adds delay before any appointment
Strong deadline-driven urgency for expedited handling plus clear precedents (MHP 2016, CHP İstanbul) give court a model
Court may defer/postpone given politically explosive nature and pending 'absolute nullity' litigation

reasoning:
**Forecast: 22% (NO favored)**

The question asks whether a Turkish civil court will appoint a national-level çağrı heyeti for CHP between June 27 – July 11, 2026. As of June 30, the application wasn't even confirmed filed and no ruling had been issued. Three hurdles must all clear within ~12 days: filing, a ruling before July 11, and a *favorable appointment* (not rejection/postponement/bilirkişi-only). The typical ~15-day processing time would push the median ruling past the window, and the disputed signature count likely triggers an expert examination first (explicitly excluded by fine print). Strong deadline-driven urgency and clear precedents provide upward pressure, but the modal outcome remains "court has not yet ruled by July 11" — yielding **0.22**.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, between June 27, 2026 and July 11, 2026 (inclusive), a Turkish Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi (Civil Court of Peace) issues a decision appointing a "çağrı heyeti" (call committee) charged with convening an extraordinary congress (olağanüstü kurultay) of the Republican People's Party (CHP), as reported by [BBC News Türkçe](https://www.bbc.com/turkce) or other [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). It resolves NO if no such appointment decision is reported within that window (including if the court rejects the application, declines jurisdiction, postpones, or has not yet ruled).
fine_print: "Appointment" means a court decision that names or orders the constitution of a çağrı heyeti for the CHP at the national/general (genel merkez) level; it counts even if the decision is later appealed or stayed, provided the appointment itself has been issued within the window. A court decision concerning only a provincial (il) or district (ilçe) organization does not count. A mere acceptance of the application for filing, an interim/expert (bilirkişi) examination of the signatures, or a verification of signatures without an actual çağrı heyeti appointment does not count. If credible reporting is conflicting about whether an appointment decision was issued within the window, the question resolves based on the preponderance of credible reporting as of July 11, 2026, 23:59 Europe/Istanbul time; if it remains genuinely unclear, it resolves NO.

factors.description: Status quo: no application confirmed filed and no ruling as of June 30; question resolves NO if court hasn't ruled by July 11
Tight timeline: applications typically ~15 days; if filed June 29-30 the median ruling lands ~July 14, after the July 11 window
Likely two-stage process: court probably orders bilirkişi signature examination first (disputed 833 vs 820), which fine print says does NOT count and adds delay before any appointment

Strong deadline-driven urgency: Özel's camp needs ruling by ~July 11 for 15-day notice to July 26 congress; lawyers will request expedited handling, and precedent (MHP 2016, CHP İstanbul) gives court a clear model
Court may defer/postpone given politically explosive nature and pending higher-court 'absolute nullity' litigation, using recess as convenient excuse

input.anchor: Base rate for a specific contested court ruling occurring within a ~2-week window when not yet filed: low, ~25%
input.assessment: Multiple hurdles must all clear within a ~12-day window: (1) application actually filed, (2) court rules before July 11, (3) ruling is a favorable national-level appointment (not reject/postpone/bilirki\u015fi-only). The conditional chain (~0.90 x 0.40 x 0.60 ~ 0.22) and the fresh, unfiled status both point to a low-20s probability. The biggest single likely outcome is 'court has not yet ruled by July 11.' Urgency and precedent provide upward pressure but typical processing time plus a probable signature-examination step push the appointment past the window.
input.calibration_notes: Status quo favors NO. Not hedging toward 50% \u2014 evidence (unfiled, typical 15-day timeline exceeding window, likely interim examination step) genuinely supports low-20s. Urgency keeps it from being lower.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the court expedites given deadline pressure; whether it orders a bilirki\u015fi step first; whether it appoints vs rejects/postpones.
input.tool_audit: research() returned a rich, well-sourced summary with timeline anchors and multiple Turkish news sources. Sufficient for this forecast; no failures.
input.process_reflection: The research sub-agent worked well for this fast-moving foreign-language political question, aggregating many Turkish-language sources into a coherent timeline. No friction. A conditional-chain decomposition could have been spawned via subforecast but the question is simple enough to reason through directly.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified all five factors against the research trace and two fresh/related worldview entries. The forecast is well-grounded.

GROUNDING CHECKS (all pass):
- Factor 1 (no application confirmed filed, no ruling as of June 30): Directly supported — trace states "Announced and prepared, but NOT independently confirmed as filed" and "No Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi ruling... had been reported as of 30 June 2026."
- Factor 2 (~15-day timeline → median ruling ~July 14): Supported by research ("Ruling expected within ~15 days").
- Factor 3 (likely bilirkişi signature-examination step first): Inferential but legitimately grounded — the disputed signature count (833 vs 820) and the fine-print exclusion of bilirkişi are both in the trace. The "probably" hedge is appropriate; not a hallucination.
- Factor 4 (deadline-driven urgency, +0.9): Supported (July 11 operative deadline per Türkiye Gazetesi, 15-day notice → July 26 congress, July 20 recess, MHP 2016 / CHP İstanbul precedents). Positive sign correctly favors YES.
- Factor 5 (court may defer pending higher-court litigation): Supported by the "mutlak butlan" litigation in trace.

No double-counting (each factor draws on a distinct mechanism), no wrong-direction signs, no resolution misalignment (factors engage the specific terms: national-level only, bilirkişi exclusion, June 27–July 11 window). No pre-publication-event problem — nothing resolving occurred in the June 27–30 pre-publication window. Worldview entries (cca9e611 fresh, 5d200b42 superseded) are consistent: no court ruling, ~15-day expectation, recess and signature disputes all corroborated. No cross-question contradiction.

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate matches. Conditional chain (P(filed)~0.9 × P(rules in window)~0.4 × P(favorable appointment)~0.6 ≈ 0.22) is sound; "court has not yet ruled by July 11" is the correct modal outcome given typical processing time plus a probable signature-examination step. Anchor (~25%) and final (23%) are close — no unjustified anchor divergence. Counterargument is genuinely adversarial (hard-deadline → expedited handling → precedent favors granting; specifies docket number / expedited hearing as mind-changers). Self-confidence 0.65 is calibrated to the unfiled, fluid status.

ONE MINOR NOTE (not forecast-affecting): The tool_audit narrative says research "returned a rich, well-sourced summary... no failures," but ground-truth metrics show research had 1 error (50% error rate across 2 calls). The trace confirms the first research call failed on a schema validation error ("questions: Field required") and was retried successfully. This was a self-corrected input error, not a substantive research gap, and the eventual result was complete — so it does not affect the forecast. Worth noting only for accuracy of the audit claim.

Verdict: approve.
